hero.description: Share how you work, what kind of support you offer, and how clients can reach you without turning your profile into another generic directory page.

how.title: Three clear steps for therapists
how.description: A calmer path from profile setup to a public page that already carries more context.

steps.title: Describe how you work
steps.text: Answer a short therapist onboarding flow with structured questions and free-text responses about your style, focus, and fit.

steps.title: Sign in and publish instantly
steps.text: Create your account after the quiz so we can store your answers, build your profile, and make it live right away.

steps.title: Keep your profile current
steps.text: Update your English source content, review the Finnish prefill, add media URLs, and adjust availability from the dashboard.

comparison.title: Less directory noise, more real context

testimonials.title: A profile that sounds more like you
testimonials.description: A few reasons this flow is built to feel closer to real practice than a generic listing.

faq.title: What therapists usually want to know
faq.description: The practical details before you begin.
faq.ctaDescription: Start with the therapist onboarding flow, then continue into auth only when you're ready to save and publish your profile.

directoryPoints: Profiles often flatten your working style into a few broad labels
Clients arrive with little context and compare you in isolation
Updating copy, links, and availability becomes another admin task
Localized content is hard to maintain consistently
myotaPoints: Clients arrive with richer context from the match flow
Your profile includes structured fit signals and free-text nuance
English can stay your source language while Finnish is prefilled
Media links, booking links, and availability live in one dashboard

testimonials.quote: This feels closer to how I actually describe my work than a standard profile form ever has.
testimonials.name: Laura, integrative therapist

testimonials.quote: I like that the client arrives with some context already. It changes the feeling of the first contact.
testimonials.name: Mikko, psychotherapist
testimonials.context: Using the dashboard to keep availability current

testimonials.quote: Being able to write in English first and refine the Finnish version afterwards makes the whole setup much lighter.
testimonials.name: Sofia, trauma-informed therapist

faqs.question: Do I need to sign up before I answer the therapist quiz?
faqs.answer: No. You answer the onboarding flow first. Authentication only appears when you are ready to save and publish what you wrote.

faqs.question: Can I edit my profile after it goes live?
faqs.answer: Yes. The dashboard is designed for ongoing edits to your public profile, localized content, media links, and availability.

faqs.question: Do I need to provide video files directly in the platform?
faqs.answer: No. The first version uses external URLs for intro videos, booking links, and other public media.

faqs.question: How does Finnish localization work?
faqs.answer: English is your source language. Finnish can be prefilled for you and then adjusted manually inside the dashboard.
